cassette: none: reducer.PostgresSupplyChainImpactWriter operates over []SupplyChainImpactFinding Go values, not a CanonicalMaterialization; the two fixture findings live inline in go/internal/replay/costcounting/supply_chain_impact_cost_test.go
description: Two affected_exact impact findings for distinct CVEs in one scope, driven through the production Postgres fact writer over an InstrumentedDB-wrapped counting fake. WriteSupplyChainImpactFindings now calls the shared reducerBatchInsertVersionedFacts bounded chunked bulk insert (go/internal/reducer/reducer_fact_batch_insert.go, batch size 1000, issue #5317) instead of one ExecContext per finding: two rows fit one chunk, so eshu_dp_postgres_query_duration_seconds records exactly 1 write observation and statements_executed=1. Any increase — an N+1 write-per-finding regression — trips the gate; the companion N+1 negative control test proves this by calling the writer once per finding and asserting the count exceeds this budget.
refresh_path: manual: edit the inline fixture findings in supply_chain_impact_cost_test.go and this file together, reviewed in the same diff (no credentialed cassette refresh applies since there is no cassette)
